   "created_at": "2022-02-10T15:03:13Z",
   "github_updated_at": "2026-02-21T10:23:15Z",
   "body_snippet": "Hi all,\r\nI'm using mRemoteNG since YESTERDAY because I had issues with Windows standard RDP connection (freeze every 1 min).\r\nWith mRemoteNG, same server, same user, same PC >>> ZERO PROBLEMS... PERFECT...\r\n\r\nBut I noticed mRemoteNG had a few limitations:\r\n- no way to open a specific connection at startup\r\n- no way to auto-close mRemoteNG after this connection have been closed\r\n\r\n\r\nSo I wrote a BAT script to make the following: \r\n-1- search the string Connected=\"false\" in the config file ...\\App...",
-  "our_status": "new",
-  "priority": null,
+  "our_status": "triaged",
+  "priority": "P3-enhancement",
   "target_release": null,
   "our_branch": null,
   "our_pr": null,
-  "iterations": [],
+  "iterations": [
+    {
+      "seq": 1,
+      "date": "2026-02-23",
+      "type": "triaged",
+      "description": "Implement new feature to allow autostarting a specified connection via command-line argument and closing the application when that connection terminates."
+    }
+  ],
   "comments": [
     {
       "id": "IC_kwDOAAcIMM5iUP2Z",
@@ -42,5 +49,5 @@
   "needs_action": true,
   "waiting_for_us": false,
   "last_synced": "2026-02-21T12:06:48Z",
-  "notes": ""
+  "notes": "AI triage (gemini): Implement new feature to allow autostarting a specified connection via command-line argument and closing the application when that connection terminates.\nApproach: Introduce a new command-line argument (e.g., --autostart-connection) to specify a connection by name. Implement logic in StartupArgumentsInterpreter and FrmMain to parse this argument, initiate the specified connection, and subscribe to the ConnectionInitiator.ConnectionClosed event. Upon closure of the autostarted connection, and if no other active connections exist, trigger Application.Exit(). Add a new option to control if other connections should prevent the application from closing."
 }

diff --git a/mRemoteNG/Tools/Cmdline/StartupArgumentsInterpreter.cs b/mRemoteNG/Tools/Cmdline/StartupArgumentsInterpreter.cs
index 7bbd8dfc9..6e422cf80 100644
--- a/mRemoteNG/Tools/Cmdline/StartupArgumentsInterpreter.cs
+++ b/mRemoteNG/Tools/Cmdline/StartupArgumentsInterpreter.cs
@@ -29,10 +29,17 @@ namespace mRemoteNG.Tools.Cmdline
         /// </summary>
         public static string? StartupConnectTo { get; private set; }
 
+        /// <summary>
+        /// When true, mRemoteNG exits automatically after all connections are closed.
+        /// Enabled by the --exit-on-disconnect (or --eod) command-line argument.
+        /// </summary>
+        public static bool ExitOnDisconnect { get; private set; }
+
         public static void ResetConnectionArgs()
         {
             ConnectTo = null;
             StartupConnectTo = null;
+            ExitOnDisconnect = false;
         }
 
         public StartupArgumentsInterpreter(MessageCollector messageCollector)
@@ -59,6 +66,7 @@ namespace mRemoteNG.Tools.Cmdline
                 ParseCustomConnectionPathArg(args);
                 ParseConnectArg(args);
                 ParseStartupConnectArg(args);
+                ParseExitOnDisconnectArg(args);
             }
             catch (Exception ex)
             {
@@ -155,5 +163,12 @@ namespace mRemoteNG.Tools.Cmdline
             _messageCollector.AddMessage(MessageClass.DebugMsg, $"Cmdline arg: startup auto-connect to \"{startupConnectValue}\"");
             StartupConnectTo = startupConnectValue;
         }
+
+        private void ParseExitOnDisconnectArg(CmdArgumentsInterpreter args)
+        {
+            if (args["exit-on-disconnect"] == null && args["eod"] == null) return;
+            _messageCollector.AddMessage(MessageClass.DebugMsg, "Cmdline arg: Exit when all connections are closed");
+            ExitOnDisconnect = true;
+        }
     }
 }
